MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-21-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ENVIROPROTECT, LLC'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WARNING THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). AMENDED ORDER AND AMENDED JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ENVIROPROTECT, LLC, IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff EnviroProtect, LLC's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

amended order and amended judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
